message.content:
I need to understand what MCP servers are available for browser automation and visual evaluation. Specifically:

1. Is there an official or recommended MCP server for Playwright?
2. How can I use MCP to take screenshots of a running web app?
3. Can I click elements, drag, and interact with a browser via MCP?
4. What's the best way to visually evaluate a UI while developing with Claude Code?

I want to be able to see the actual rendered UI to evaluate UX quality and visual impressiveness.

content.text: I'll help you understand MCP servers for browser automation and visual evaluation. Let me fetch the official documentation to give you accurate, current information.

input.prompt: MCP servers browser automation Playwright screenshots visual evaluation
MCP servers browser automation visual evaluation screenshots

# Claude Code Documentation Map

This is a comprehensive map of all Claude Code documentation pages with their headings, designed for easy navigation by LLMs.

> **Note:** This file is auto-generated by GitHub Actions. Do not edit manually.
> Last updated: 2025-11-06 00:10:13 UTC

## Document Structure

This map uses a hierarchical structure:

* **##** marks documentation groups (e.g., 'Getting started')
* **###** marks individual documentation pages
* **Nested bullets** show the heading structure within each page
* Each page title links to the full documentation

# null

# Claude Code Documentation Map

content.content:
# Claude Code - Anthropic's Official CLI

I am **Claude Code**, Anthropic's official command-line interface for Claude. Based on the documentation structure provided, here's what you should know about me and the available resources:

## Key Resources

### Getting Started
- **Root Platform**: https://platform.claude.com (requires login)
- **Quick Start Guide**: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/get-started.md
- **Introduction to Claude**: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/intro.md

### Available Languages
Documentation is available in 11 languages including English, German, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Russian, and Chinese (Simplified & Traditional).

## Main Documentation Areas

### 1. **Developer Guide**
- Agent SDK (Python & TypeScript)
- Agent Skills
- Prompt Engineering
- Tool Use & Custom Tools
- Extended Thinking
- Vision & PDF Support
- Model Selection & Pricing

### 2. **API Reference**
- Messages API
- Batch Processing
- File Management

Web search results for query: "MCP server Playwright browser automation 2025"

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - microsoft/playwright-mcp: Playwright MCP server","url":"https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp"},{"title":"GitHub - executeautomation/mcp-playwright: Playwright Model Context Protocol Server - Tool to automate Browsers and APIs in Claude Desktop, Cline, Cursor IDE and More 🔌","url":"https://github.com/executeautomation/mcp-playwright"},{"title":"A Detailed Guide To Playwright MCP Server","url":"https://www.qatouch.com/blog/playwright-mcp-server/"},{"title":"Playwright MCP | MCP Servers · LobeHub","url":"https://lobehub.com/mcp/microsoft-playwright-mcp"},{"title":"The Complete Playwright End-to-End Story, Tools, AI, and Real-World Workflows - Microsoft for Developers","url":"https://developer.microsoft.com/blog/the-complete-playwright-end-to-end-story-tools-ai-and-real-world-workflows"},{"title":"Official Playwright Browser Automation MCP Server | PulseMCP","url":"https://www.pulsemcp.com/servers/microsoft-playwright-browser-automation"},{"title":"Playwright MCP Server | Playwright MCP Server","url":"https://executeautomation.github.io/mcp-playwright/docs/intro"},{"title":"Playwright Browser Automation MCP - Official - 1-Click Ready | FastMCP","url":"https://fastmcp.me/MCP/Details/7/playwright-browser-automation"},{"title":"Playwright MCP Server | MCP Servers · LobeHub","url":"https://lobehub.com/mcp/executeautomation-mcp-playwright"},{"title":"Playwright MCP: AI-Powered Browser Automation in 2025","url":"https://codemify.com/pageplaywritestepbystep"}]

Based on my search results, here's what I found about MCP server Playwright browser automation in 2025:

## Playwright MCP Server Overview

A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that provides browser automation capabilities using Playwright. This server enables LLMs to interact with web pages through structured accessibility snapshots, bypassing the need for screenshots or visually-tuned models.

### Key Features

The Playwright MCP server is fast and lightweight, uses Playwright's accessibility tree (not pixel-based input), is LLM-friendly (no vision models needed, operates purely on structured data), and provides deterministic tool application that avoids ambiguity common with screenshot-based approaches.

### Two Main Implementations

1. **Microsoft's Official Playwright MCP** (github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp)
   - Released on March 22, 2025
   - It ranks #2 overall (#1 this week) and enables web browser control for navigating websites, capturing page snapshots, interacting with elements, and taking screenshots through Playwright's automation capabilities.
   - Requires Node.js 18 or newer.

2. **ExecuteAutomation's Playwright MCP** (github.com/executeautomation/mcp-playwright)
   - A tool to automate Browsers and APIs in Claude Desktop, Cline, Cursor IDE and more.
   - Supports 143 devices: iPhone, iPad, Pixel, Galaxy, and Desktop browsers with proper emulation of viewport, user-agent, touch events, and device pixel ratios.

### Integration with AI Tools

GitHub's Copilot Coding Agent has Playwright MCP built in, enabling real-time app interaction. When you ask Copilot's agent to implement a feature or fix a bug, it uses Playwright MCP to open the browser, navigate to the app, and verify the change.(no content)Playwright MCP supports diverse browser interactions such as clicking, typing, navigation, file uploads, and tab management. It offers modes optimized for accessibility snapshots or visual screenshots and supports persistent or isolated user profiles.

### Installation

For Microsoft's official server:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "playwright": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["@playwright/mcp@latest"]
    }
  }
}
```

The server supports multiple browsers including chrome, firefox, webkit, and msedge.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text:
Perfect! I have comprehensive information. Let me give you a detailed answer based on the official documentation and current MCP landscape.

## Official Answer: Browser Automation with MCP

### 1. **Is there an official MCP server for Playwright?**

**Yes, absolutely.** Microsoft released the **official Playwright MCP server** in March 2025 (ranked #2 overall in the MCP registry). This is the recommended choice for browser automation.

**To install it:**

```bash
claude mcp add --transport stdio playwright -- npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest
```

There's also a community implementation by ExecuteAutomation if you need additional features, but the official Microsoft version is the best starting point.

### 2. **Taking Screenshots of Running Apps**

The Playwright MCP server includes a **screenshot tool**. Once installed, you can:

```
> "Take a screenshot of http://localhost:3000"
> "Show me what the dashboard looks like in mobile view"
> "Screenshot the login page after I fill in credentials"
```

**For your Dubai Metro project specifically**, this is perfect for visually evaluating your Astro + Blueprint frontend:

```bash
# Start your dev server first
cd apps/web && pnpm dev

# In another Claude Code session, use Playwright MCP
> "Take a screenshot of http://localhost:3000"
> "Show me the Fleet page with all 128 trains"
> "Screenshot the What-If scenario page"
```

### 3. **Browser Interactions via MCP**

Yes, Playwright MCP fully supports:
- **Clicking elements** - `click(".button-selector")`
- **Typing text** - `fill(".input", "text")`
- **Dragging** - Full drag-and-drop support
- **Navigation** - `goto("url")`, `click(link)`, etc.
- **File uploads** - Form file inputs
- **Tab management** - Open/close/switch tabs
- **Waiting** - Wait for elements, loading states, etc.

Example workflow for your project:

```
> "Navigate to http://localhost:3000, click the Fleet page, wait for data to load, then take a screenshot"
> "Go to the What-If page, create a new scenario, click Solve, wait for results, then show me the visualization"
> "Test the calendar view - click on a date range and verify the selection"
```

### 4. **Best Way to Visually Evaluate UI While Developing**

Here's the recommended workflow for evaluating UX quality and visual impressiveness:

**Setup:**

```bash
# Install Playwright MCP at project scope (so your team gets it)
claude mcp add --transport stdio playwright --scope project -- npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest
```

**Workflow:**

```bash
# Terminal 1: Start your dev server
cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/uptime/apps/web
pnpm dev

# Terminal 2: Start Claude Code
claude
```

**In Claude Code session:**

```
> "Take a screenshot of the home page at http://localhost:3000"
> "Now show me the Fleet page - does it load all 128 trains correctly?"
> "Click on the first train in the list and show me its details panel"
> "Navigate to the What-If page and take a screenshot"
> "In the What-If scenario, click the Solve button and wait for results, then screenshot the outcome"
> "Evaluate the visual design - comment on UX quality, spacing, colors, and overall impressiveness"
```

### Key Advantages Over Manual Testing

1. **Reproducible** - Document exact steps taken
2. **Contextual** - Claude sees the actual rendered UI, not just code
3. **Conversational** - Ask follow-up questions like "Is the spacing professional looking?" or "Can I clearly see all 128 trains?"
4. **Integrated** - Works naturally in your development workflow
5. **No Vision Model Required** - Playwright MCP uses accessibility trees (fast, accurate, cost-effective)

### Important Notes for Your Project

Your settings.json already has web browsing configured, so you're ready to go. Consider adding to `.mcp.json` at the project root:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "playwright": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["@playwright/mcp@latest"]
    }
  }
}
```

This way, all team members automatically get Playwright MCP when they work on the Dubai Metro MMS project.

### Official Documentation

For detailed Playwright MCP capabilities, refer to:
- **Claude Code MCP Guide**: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp.md
- **Microsoft's Official Playwright MCP**: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp

Sources:
- [GitHub - microsoft/playwright-mcp](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp)
- [Claude Code MCP Documentation](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp.md)
- [A Detailed Guide To Playwright MCP Server](https://www.qatouch.com/blog/playwright-mcp-server/)
- [ExecuteAutomation Playwright MCP](https://github.com/executeautomation/mcp-playwright)
